rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Allowing Additional Time To Complete Deposition Of Jack Healy
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DGP ASSOCIATES LP Motion For Order Allowing Additional Time To Complete Deposition Of Jack Healy. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Plaintiff may depose Mr. Healey for a total of fourteen hours inclusive of the hours already consumed. Plaintiff noticed the deposition to last for two days and was prejudiced by the fact that Defendant did not inform Plaintiff of any time limitations until well into the second (partial) day of the deposition. While this was not noticed as a PMK deposition, Plaintiff contends, without contradiction, that Mr. Healey was the person most knowledgeable about key subjects. A PMK deposition may last 14 hours. The parties shall meet and confer within 10 days to determine, in good faith, how many hours of active questioning at deposition have already occurred. The court is unable to make that determination based on the record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re: Pltf'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT HAROLD WONG, WILLIAM YEE AND ASSOCIATES Motion For Protective Order Re: Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for a protective order is denied. As a cross-complainant, Harold Wong waives his 5th Amendment privilege as to the factual issues tendered by his cross-complaint. (See Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Superior Court (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 554, 557 and cases cited therein; see also Weil & Brown, Civ. Proc Before Trial 8:134 (citing Fremont) ["[Cross-complainant] cannot have his cake and eat it too."].) No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK PARKER VS. BENTLY HOLDINGS CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT BENTLY HOLDINGS CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel. Motion denied. Defendants' delay in bringing this Motion was unreasonable and the ensuing prejudice to Plaintiff if this Motion was granted is extreme. While Defendants may have warned Plaintiff's counsel that its actions were grounds for disqualification one day after the Complaint was filed, the Court was not notified that Defendants believed the actions required disqualification until over a year later. Furthermore, if Defendants believed that Plaintiff's counsel was so tainted by its possession of privileged material, it is inconceivable that Defendants would have chosen to engage in settlement discussions while Plaintiff was represented by the same counsel. Finally, Plaintiff has shown that he would be severely prejudiced if Defendants' Motion was granted due to additional delay in the litigation of his claim on its merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC. A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION, SCOTT REAY, JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Chuck Boneck was dismissed as a defendant on July 18, 2014. As such, the relevant inquiry is whether Plaintiffs properly brought DFEH claims against Defendant Principal Builders, Inc., because the allegations about Mr. Boneck pertain to the failure to prevent harassment claim against Principal Builders. The Court already considered and rejected Defendants' argument that the DFEH claims are defective in ruling on Defendants' demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"). The allegations against Mr. Boneck in the SAC are therefore not improper or irrelevant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JONES JR VS. HUGO GAMBOA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT HUGO GAMBOA's Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default. Granted. (CCP sec. 473(b).) "[I]t has been held that where the aggrieved party makes a strong showing of diligence in seeking relief after discovery of facts, and the other party is unable to show prejudice from the delay, the original negligence in allowing the default to be taken will be excused on a weak showing." (Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 740.) Defendant has demonstrated diligence, as he has moved to set aside the default within two months of its entry. Plaintiff has not established that he will be prejudiced if the default is set aside. Defendant has established excusable mistake and/or neglect by relying on Plaintiff's successor-in-interest's representation that the lawsuit would not be pursued. Defendant shall pay Plaintiff's costs for filing and service in the amount of $937.54 within 30 days of entry of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VALLEY CENTER EQUITY HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE et al VS. GCL-SOL ORCHARD HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Third Party Subpoena Of Gcl-Sol Orchard Holdings, Llc (Special Appearance); Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 4, OTHER NORTH AMERICAN DEVELOPMENT BANK (NON-PARTY) Motion To Quash Third Party Subpoena Of Gcl-Sol Orchard Holdings, LLC (Special Appearance); Appendix. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice. The moving party may, with proper notice, bring a motion to quash the amended subpoena, which is the only one at issue here. Before bringing another motion to quash in this court, the parties should consider whether or not such a motion may be governed by the Interstate Depositions and Discovery Act which apparently requires a motion to quash to be brought in the discovery state (here it is Texas). The parties have not briefed, and the court does not know whether Texas has adopted Interstate Depositions and Discovery Act, or, if not, what procedures might be in effect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FOUZIA HDOUBANE VS. GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTs GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC., JOHN KENASTON, RENATE KENASTON, GABRIELE KENASTON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Plaintiff concedes that causes of action 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10, 15, and 19 are deficient, and as to those causes of action the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to cause of action 8, which presumptively either Plaintiff intended to include in its list of admittedly deficient causes of action or otherwise does not oppose Defendants' grounds for demur. The Demurrer is also sustained with 10 days leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to clearly allege whether the contract at issue is written or oral. The Demurrer for uncertainty as to causes of action 1-12 and 14-20 is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to indicate which causes of action are brought against which Defendant(s), and to sufficiently allege a basis for liability against each Defendant for each cause of action. The Demurrer is also sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the uncertainty of which Wage Order allegedly applies to the employment relationship. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st and 4th causes of action. Plaintiff must plead in compliance with Oppenheimer v. Moebius (1957) 151 Cal.App.2d 818. As to the 6th cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege an injury arising from the missing wage statements. The Demurrer as to the 11th cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the Labor Code violation(s) that is the basis for this claim and the basis for Plaintiff's entitlement to restitution. As to the 12th cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff no longer works for Defendant and therefore would not suffer personal injury from continuing Labor Code violations. The Demurrer is overruled as to causes of action 14, 16, 17, 18 and 20 as the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support each claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
